NEW DELHI (Metro Rail News): Taking a major step towards a sustainable and green mode of transportation, Indian Railways is all set to launch its first indigenously built hydrogen train on 17th July. Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi will flag-off the train in Haryana from Jind Railway Station. A senior Railway officer said that they’ve received the confirmation from the Prime Minister’s Office that the first Hydrogen Train will be flagged off by the Prime Minister on 17th July from Jind. After the launch, the train will start its commercial operations, covering 356 km every day and making two 89 km circular trips between Jind and Sonipat. The maintenance depot is situated at Shakurbasti in Delhi. TUV SUD, an independent international testing and certification organisation, carried out the safety and design inspections. As per the security protocols, the hydrogen power system will be turned off bringing the train to a “dead” condition, from there a diesel locomotive will shift it to the maintenance facility. The train is equipped with eight passenger coaches consisting of 682 seats and will facilitate around 2,600 passengers.The designed maximum speed of the train is 110 kmph but the operations will be carried out at 75 kmph on the Jind-Sonipat route. This train features two Driving Power Cars (DPC) positioned at either end of the 10-coach trainset (one at the front and one at the back). The eight passenger coaches are located in the middle. The total traction capacity stands at 2,400 kW (approximately 3,200 hp), as each DPC can generate 1,200 kilowatts (kW) of power (1,600 hp) which will be enough for the train to operate smoothly. At peak capacity the train is expected to use around 300 kg of hydrogen each day. The train will be powered by hydrogen fuel cells that convert compressed hydrogen into electricity. This train is designed to store 440 kg of compressed hydrogen. Being highly sustainable and environmentally friendly it only emits water as a byproduct. The commencement of the first hydrogen train will mark a monumental mileston

DELHI (Metro Rail News): The Delhi Metro Rail Corporation (DMRC) has created a fully-owned subsidiary, Delhi Metro International Limited (DMIL), to broaden its global transit consultancy and operations business. The subsidiary was renamed to accommodate a major expansion of its scope and objectives. DMRC issued a fresh statement highlighting the full activation and global ambitions of DMIL on July 7, 2026. DMIL is now ready to execute projects globally, leveraging DMRC’s proven excellence. This formation allows DMIL to build upon DMRC’s over 20 years of experience managing one of the world’s most esteemed metro systems. It will execute consultancy, project execution, operations and maintenance (O&M), and advisory services for metro, railway, and urban transit projects across India and abroad. Delhi Metro currently operates a network of more than 416 km with over 300 stations, serving over 6.5 million passengers on a daily basis. It also manages operations and maintenance for metro systems in several other Indian cities, such as Mumbai Metro Line 3, Chennai Metro Phase 2, Patna Metro, Noida-Greater Noida Metro, and Rapid Metro Gurugram. Furthermore, it successfully operates seamless driverless metro operations on Magenta & Pink Lines of the Delhi Metro network, predictive maintenance, and multimodal integration. The launch of this new subsidiary is a strategic move to monetize DMRC’s proven capabilities on a global scale. This renaming reflects the desire of Indian infrastructure firms to export their engineering and project management skills to global markets, especially in urban transport, where many countries are pouring resources into metro and mass transit projects. “Building on over two decades of expertise in developing and operating one of the world’s most respected metro systems, Delhi Metro Rail Corporation (DMRC) has established Delhi Metro International Limited (DMIL),” the company mentioned in its press release. KOCHI (Metro Rail News): Kochi Metro Rail Limited (KMRL) is planning to expand the water metro network from Aluva Metro Station to Cochin International Airport (CIAL) through the Periyar River. They have signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) with the SCMS Water Institute to assess the feasibility of the proposed metro extension. Under the MoU, the institute will serve as a collaborator for the project , providing research and technical support on the feasibility assessment of the project. The institute will conduct various scientific analysis and assessments to test the feasibility of the project. In a significant step towards expanding Kerala's sustainable urban mobility network, KMRL has signed a MoU with the SCMS Water Institute to collaborate on the feasibility study for the proposed Water Metro extension connecting Aluva Metro Station to CIAL through the Periyar River pic.twitter.com/1xp3dCbXHj Earlier, KMRL issued a tender to conduct a comprehensive topographical assessment of the 4 km route from Aluva to CIAL. According to KMRL, the project will be a major step towards expanding Kerala’s sustainable urban mobility network. The approximately 4-km water route will cover major locations before reaching the airport. It is expected to link Aswathy Nagar, Naduvannoor, Pattoorkunnu, and Kalady before reaching the airport. Upon completion, the project will become a major link providing seamless connectivity to the airport via the water metro system. Kochi Water Metro is India’s first water metro system. It envisions the development of 15 identified routes which will connect 10 islands along a 78 km network. The metro involves a fleet of 78 fast, electrically propelled ferries to provide seamless connectivity between the route. This project intends to enhance connectivity with environment friendly and energy efficient technology. Also Read: PM Modi dedicates Churu Rail Doubling Projects worth ₹900 crore

RAJASTHAN (Metro Rail News): PM Modi virtually dedicated the 58 km Churu–Sadulpur and 46 km Churu–Ratangarh rail-doubling projects on July 4, 2026. He addressed the event from Balotra, Rajasthan. The rail-doubling projects cost around ₹900 crore. Covering a total of 104 km, these projects will boost rail connectivity in northwest Rajasthan improving capacity for passenger and freight transport, reducing congestion, and fostering regional investment and job growth. The live event was the host site for a massive, centralized, state-level development mega-event. During the event, PM Modi launched multiple development projects worth over ₹1 lakh crore across Rajasthan and Gujarat. Hon'ble Prime Minister Shri @narendramodi to dedicate the #Churu – #Ratangarh and Churu– #Sadulpur Rail Line Doubling Projects to the nation. This development marks another significant step towards strengthening rail connectivity and enhancing train operations in Rajasthan.… pic.twitter.com/4DuJO8IMA0 The newly upgraded corridors will allow smoother and more frequent train operations between regions. Several daily express and superfast services operate on this route: the Jodhpur–Delhi Sarai Rohilla Superfast connects Delhi and Rajasthan and the Howrah–Barmer Superfast links West Bengal and Rajasthan, Both trains link the towns of Sadulpur and Churu in about an hour. The main issue before construction was the single-line bottleneck. The project started around January 2024. Major civil, track, and related works including the doubling of the 58 km Churu–Sadulpur and 46 km Churu–Ratangarh rail lines are finished. The Railway authority highlighted the project’s benefits for smoother passenger and freight movement. Also Read: DMRC urges to implement Kerala High Speed Rail Project

NEW DELHI (Metro Rail News): Kerala is soon expected to join India’s future bullet train map. An expert committee of 4 members was appointed by UDFC to examine the proposed Thiruvananthapuram-Kannur high-speed rail project based on an interim report prepared by the “Metro Man of India”, E. Sreedharan. The expert committee is led by the secretary, transport (railways), including railway expert J. Vinayan, finance and environment experts, Dr C. Veeramani and Sridhar Radhakrishnan. The report will be submitted this week. As stated in a government order issued by the Transport Department, DMRC has prepared and submitted an interim report for the proposed high speed rail project. The Kerala High Speed Rail Project is planned as a 473.20-km standard-gauge corridor connecting Thiruvananthapuram and Kannur. DMRC has briefed the Kerala Government about the project implementation plans and urged the state to assign the project to DMRC on a turnkey basis highlighting the successful implementation of the Kochi Metro project. DMRC has assured the state that the project will be completed within five years without delays as the entire line is planned to be constructed fully elevated or underground as mentioned in the interim report, significantly decreasing surface land requirements and minimising local protests. The Kerala High Speed Rail Project is expected to link all four airports in Kerala and facilitate public transit to regions with no rail connectivity. A Delhi Metro Rail Corporation (DMRC) spokesperson said, “Crucially, this line can easily be integrated into the cross-country high-speed network managed by NHSRCL, ensuring that Kerala does not remain isolated from India’s future bullet map”. The estimated cost of the entire corridor is ₹57,000 crore, with the final completion cost of approximately Rs 60,000 crore. According to DMRC, the project will eventually have the capacity to carry 2.28 lakh passengers a day. The energy requirements of the entire corridor will be completed by captive solar power. NAGPUR (Metro Rail News): Central Railway has started using the Standard Kavach Automatic Train Protection (ATP) system on the Godhani-Teegaon section of the Nagpur Division. This step will enhance train safety on one of the busiest and most difficult railway routes while lowering the chances of accidents caused by human error. The Godhani-Teegaon section is part of the busy Itarsi-Nagpur route. This area includes a challenging ghat section with steep slopes, heavy train traffic, and complex operating conditions. Central Railway officials said that Standard Kavach will make operations for passenger and freight trains safer and more efficient along this route. Standard Kavach is a indigenous Automatic Train Protection (ATP) system. It continuously tracks train movement and assists loco pilots during operations. The system can: These features help lower the risk of accidents caused by human error and improve overall train safety. The Godhani-Teegaon section serves many passenger and freight trains each day. The steep grades and tough terrain make train operations more difficult than usual. By introducing Standard Kavach on this section, Central Railway has taken another step towards making train operations safer and more dependable. Chief Minister Mr. Mohan Charan Majhi and Union Railway Minister Mr. Ashwini Vaishnaw jointly flagged off the newly Brahmapur-Udhna (Surat) Amrit Bharat Express, which marks the upgrade of the service from tri-weekly to daily operation, alongside the brand-new, tri-weekly Puri–Koraput Express. The Brahmapur–Udhna Amrit Bharat Express connects the “Silk City of Odisha,” Brahmapur (Odisha), to Udhna (Gujarat), a major business hub in Western India. The high-profile launches conducted via synchronized events at the Puri and Brahmapur railway stations are designed to directly scale up interstate labor mobility. Meanwhile, the intrastate Puri–Koraput Express connects Puri, one of Odisha’s major pilgrimage sites, to Koraput in the state’s western region. While the Brahmapur–Udhna Amrit Bharat Express runs daily, the Puri–Koraput Express operates three times a week. This dual launch emphasizes safer, more convenient travel and stronger cultural and economic ties between Odisha and Gujarat, crediting central government support under the “Double Engine” framework for a Viksit Odisha (Developed Odisha) and Viksit Bharat (Developed India). Brahmapur–Udhna daily Amrit Bharat Express: Inter-State economic corridor Amrit Bharat Express from Brahmapur(Odisha)-Udhna (Surat),train no -19021/19022 Connects Brahmapur, Silk city of Odisha to Udhna major business hub of Gujarat via other states such as Andhra Pradesh, Chhattisgarh, and Maharashtra . The Brahmapur Amrit Bharat Express operates daily, covering a distance of 1,710 km (1,063 mi) in 30 hours and 15 minutes at an average speed of 56 km/h. The train features a total of 22 coaches, including 11 General Second Class (GS) seating coaches, 8 Sleeper Class (SL) coaches, 2 Second Class-cum-Luggage Vans, and a pantry car, allowing it to accommodate more than 1,800 passengers per trip. articles.title: Foreign investors look past US uncertainty to long-term opportunity
articles.raw_summary: Overseas companies are still expanding in America even as tariffs, labour shortages and shifting policy complicate investment plans
articles.extracted_text: Roula Khalaf, Editor of the FT, selects her favourite stories in this weekly newsletter. “May you live in interesting times” is an oft-cited curse that, despite the mythology around its Chinese provenance, most probably originated in the West. There is little doubt that these times are interesting, and some of the greatest and most unpredictable changes are happening in the US, the leader of the western world. This has not dimmed the appeal of the country as a place to do business. Preliminary figures from the US Bureau of Economic Analysis show that foreign investment in America in 2025 reached $232bn, the highest level since 2021 . While acquisitions were, as always, by far the largest proportion, expansions of existing facilities also showed an increase. Manufacturing accounted for more than half of the inflows, and Japan topped the list of investor nations, injecting more than $50bn into America, nearly twice as much as second-placed Germany. New investments or expanding sectors were transportation and warehousing, computer and electronics manufacturing and chemicals manufacturing. Some content could not load. Check your internet connection or browser settings. In June, the FT hosted an event in New York to mark the publication of its fourth annual FT-Nikkei Investing in America ranking . The index scores top American cities on their attractiveness to foreign investors based on criteria such as the availability of a skilled workforce, diversity and the ease of doing business. The 2026 winner was Boston. As access to energy became a hot topic with disruptions caused by the Iran war and the increased load on the US energy system from growing data centre demand, rankings for 2026 included a new energy resilience score. This was a factor which helped Boston to the top spot, along with the city’s deep bench of intellectual capital. Energy resilience was just one of the factors influencing the appeal of America as a destination for FDI that were discussed under the Chatham House Rule by panellists from foreign-owned companies that do business across the country. Speakers at the June event gave a consistent message about the draw for foreign businesses to America. Stable economic growth, an innovative mindset and deep capital markets as well as commercial openness and the ease of doing business all combine to bring investment into a market which is “too big to ignore”. The appeal to foreign companies of US innovation and tech leadership is exemplified by the Spanish government’s decision in April 2026 to establish a biotechnology venture capital fund in Boston, stepping into a space created by the exit of US federal funds. This gives Spanish companies access to America’s intellectual capital while retaining intellectual property rights. This article is part of the Investing in America series, a signature initiative by the FT and Nikkei in the US. This collaboration highlights the key issues faced by foreign investors in the country. 🔎 Visit the Investing in America hub for more from this series For a number of European companies in sectors ranging from pharmaceuticals to consumer goods to technology, the US is the largest single market by revenues — in some instances generating more sales than the EU as a whole. This is true also for some Japanese companies — Toyota and Sony gather more revenue in the US than in their home market. articles.raw_summary: Federal Reserve announces the leadership and objectives of its task forces to advance the conduct of monetary policy

Secure .gov websites use HTTPS A lock ( Lock Locked padlock icon ) or https:// means you've safely connected to the .gov website. Share sensitive information only on official, secure websites. The Federal Reserve, the central bank of the United States, provides the nation with a safe, flexible, and stable monetary and financial system. The Federal Reserve on Thursday announced the leadership and objectives of its task forces to advance the conduct of monetary policy. "The Federal Reserve's commitment to price stability and maximum employment is unwavering. As is our resolve to pursue our mandate with rigor," said Chairman Kevin Warsh. "The U.S. economy has changed significantly over the last generation, and never more so than right now. Each task force will carefully consider whether policymakers' means and methods, analytical tools and policy approaches can be improved upon. I am honored that the best minds from a range of disciplines have agreed to work with us to sharpen our performance as an institution. The goal is straightforward: to ensure the Fed is best positioned to achieve our objectives in this consequential time." The five task forces will examine areas central to the broad conduct of monetary policy. They will be co-led by external advisersâaccomplished economists, business leaders, and former central bank practitionersâwith deep expertise in their fields. Supported by Federal Reserve staff, they will operate independently, with a mandate to follow the evidence, provide candid feedback, and produce rigorous findings for the Federal Open Market Committee. More information on the task forces and topics will be posted periodically on the Federal Reserve's website . For media inquiries, please e-mail [email protected] or call (202) 452-2955. Chairman's Task Forces for Advancing Monetary Policy

articles.raw_summary: Federal Reserve Board requests comment on a proposal to amend its requirements for banks to maintain anti-money laundering programs
articles.extracted_text: An official website of the United States Government Official websites use .gov A .gov website belongs to an official government organization in the United States. Secure .gov websites use HTTPS A lock ( Lock Locked padlock icon ) or https:// means you've safely connected to the .gov website. Share sensitive information only on official, secure websites. The Federal Reserve, the central bank of the United States, provides the nation with a safe, flexible, and stable monetary and financial system. The Federal Reserve Board on Tuesday requested comment on a proposal to amend its requirements for banks to maintain anti-money laundering programs. The amendments are intended to align with changes to anti-money laundering program requirements separately proposed by four other agencies. Among other changes, the proposal would require banks to focus their anti-money laundering resources based on risk, with more attention given to higher-risk customers and activities. The proposed amendments would also require banks to incorporate the Financial Crimes Enforcement Network's anti-money laundering priorities into their risk assessment processes. Under the proposal, once a bank has established an anti-money laundering program, the Federal Reserve would focus supervision and enforcement activities on significant failures to implement the program. Comments on the proposal are due 60 days after publication in the Federal Register . For media inquiries, please email [email protected] or call 202-452-2955. Federal Register notice: Anti-Money Laundering and Countering the Financing of Terrorism Programs

articles.title: Meeting of 10-11 June 2026
articles.extracted_text: Ms Schnabel started her presentation by noting that, since the Governing Council's previous monetary policy meeting on 29-30 April 2026, euro area financial markets had been torn between two competing developments: the unresolved conflict in the Middle East and the global artificial intelligence (AI) boom. The continued disruption to shipping in the Strait of Hormuz had reinforced expectations that oil prices would remain higher for longer, despite markedly lower near-term oil prices. Inflation fixings had declined from their high April readings but continued to hover above 3% for 2026 and above 2% for 2027. In tandem with oil prices, ECB rate expectations had moderated somewhat. However, markets still priced in around three interest rate hikes overall, while the median response in the ECB Survey of Monetary Analysts was an expectation of only two hikes. Although the war was weighing on growth expectations in the euro area and globally, investors’ risk appetite had remained strong. A key underlying factor had been renewed optimism about AI and strong momentum in AI-related investment. As a result, euro area equity markets had recovered close to pre-war levels, and corporate and sovereign bond spreads remained narrow. Overall, financial conditions had remained broadly unchanged since April 2026 but remained tighter than before the start of the Middle East war. Near-term oil prices had declined markedly from the peak reached at the time of the Governing Council's April monetary policy meeting. Brent crude oil prices had fallen from USD 118 to about USD 94 per barrel and had been hovering around that level since late May. At the same time, futures prices over longer horizons had remained largely insulated from the pronounced volatility observed in near-term contracts, with the latest futures curve even somewhat above the April curve and significantly above the levels recorded before the outbreak of the conflict. Gas prices had edged higher since the April meeting and continued to trade at around 50% above their pre-war levels. The impact of the Middle East conflict had extended beyond crude oil and gas prices. Since the start of the war, the prices of refined products such as petrol, diesel and jet fuel had increased by around 40-45%, significantly more than the price of oil. Prices of fertiliser-related products and plastics had also increased sharply, suggesting that higher energy costs were feeding into broader inflation by affecting downstream product prices. Food prices were expected to remain slightly higher relative to pre-war expectations and were also subject to some upside risks due to the “El Niño” event. The shifts in the oil futures curve had been mirrored in market-based inflation expectations. Inflation compensation (excluding tobacco) for 2026 and early 2027 had declined from the peaks at around the time of the Governing Council's previous monetary policy meeting. For later horizons, inflation fixings remained close to their April 2026 levels. This suggested that investors continued to expect the inflationary effects of the energy price shock to persist beyond the initial phase of the conflict, likely reflecting the expected pass-through from energy costs to other components of the pricing chain. Market prices of important means of production in circulation refer to the wholesale prices and selling prices of enterprises trading important means of production. The monitoring of market prices of important means of production in circulation includes the prices of 50 kinds of products under 9 categories. The monitoring covers nearly 2,000 wholesalers, agents, and dealers of more than 300 trading markets in 31 provinces (autonomous regions and municipalities). Price monitoring methods include information officers gathering prices on-site or requesting prices by telephone, instant messaging tools, e-mail, and so on. The number of products with price increases, decreases, and no change is counted based on the percentage change (%). articles.title: 2.Purchasing Managersâ Index for June 2026
articles.extracted_text: Department of Service Statistics of NBS of China China Federation of Logistics & Purchasing (CFLP) I. Manufacturing Purchasing Managersâ Index In June, the purchasing managersâ index (PMI) of Chinaâs manufacturing industry was 50.3%, an increase of 0.3 percentage points from the previous month and back to the expansionary range. By enterprise size, the PMI for large-sized enterprises was 50.7%, a decrease of 0.4 percentage points from the previous month, which was above the threshold; the PMI for medium sized enterprises was 50.5%, an increase of 1.9 percentage points from the previous month, which was above the threshold; the PMI for small-sized enterprises was 48.2%, a decrease of 0.3 percentage points from the previous month, which was below the threshold. By sub-index, among the five sub-indexes that constitute the manufacturing PMI, the production index and the new order index were both above the threshold, while the raw materials inventory index, the employment index and the supplier delivery time index were all below the threshold. The production index was 51.4%, an increase of 0.2 percentage points from the previous month, indicating an accelerated expansion in production activities in the manufacturing industry. The new order index was 51.2%, an increase of 1.3 percentage points from the previous month, indicating a recovery in the market demand of the manufacturing industry. The raw materials inventory index was 48.4%, a decrease of 0.2 percentage points from the previous month, indicating a continued decline in the inventories of main raw materials in the manufacturing industry. The employment index was 48.5%, a decrease of 0.1 percentage points from the previous month, indicating a slight decline in the employment climate of the manufacturing industry. The supplier delivery time index was 49.9%, an increase of 0.7 percentage points from the previous month, and still below the threshold, indicating a slight slowdown in raw material delivery times among manufacturing suppliers. By industry, the business activity index for the construction industry was 49.0%, an increase of 0.2 percentage points from the previous month; that for the service industry was 50.4%, an increase of 0.1 percentage points from the previous month. In terms of the service industry, the business activity indexes for telecommunication, radio, television and satellite transmission services, Internet software and information technology services, monetary and financial services and insurance were all in the relatively high climate range of above 55.0%; those for civil aviation transportation and real estate were all below the threshold. The new order index was 48.0%, an increase of 3.0 percentage points from the previous month, indicating a significant improvement in the market demand climate of the non-manufacturing industry. articles.title: 3.Report on the Development of Culture and Related Industries in 2025
articles.extracted_text: In 2025, under the strong leadership of the Party Central Committee with Comrade Xi Jinping at its core, all regions and departments fully implemented the decisions and plans of the Party Central Committee and the State Council. Centered on the goal of building China into a country with a strong culture, the country moved faster to nurture new-type cultural enterprises and new cultural forms, optimized the business environment, and improved the quality of development. Cultural and related industries achieved vigorous growth with steadily rising profitability. Industries with new cultural forms took the lead in driving development, and scientific and technological innovation in cultural industries picked up speed. i. Business revenue of culture and related industries exceeded 20 trillion yuan With the continuous improvement of Chinaâs cultural industrial system and market system, Chinaâs culture and related industries (hereinafter referred to as âcultural industriesâ) have expanded steadily. In 2025, cultural industries nationwide achieved a business revenue of 20,825.4 billion yuan [1] , surpassing the 20 trillion yuan threshold and hitting a record high, up by 8.8% year on year [2] . Underpinned by culture-related services, the structure of cultural industries kept improving. In 2025, culture-related services achieved a business revenue of 12,305.2 billion yuan, up by 12.8% year on year, accounting for 59.1% of the total business revenue of cultural industries, up by 2.1 percentage points year on year. Culture-related services contributed 82.7% to the business revenue growth of cultural industries. Culture-related manufacturing and culture-related wholesale and retail achieved business revenue of 4,991.9 billion yuan and 3,528.3 billion yuan, respectively, up by 0.6% and 8.0% year on year. ii. Business revenue of nine major sectors of cultural industries achieved growth across the board In 2025, business revenue saw growth across nine major sectors of cultural industries. Specifically, four sectors posted double-digit business revenue growth year on year: creative design services (13.2%), content creation and production (12.4%), news and information services (12.3%), and cultural, entertainment and leisure services (10.7%). The other five sectors recorded year-on-year growth rates as follows: 8.0% in cultural equipment production, 7.7% in cultural communication channels, 3.1% in cultural investment and operation, 2.7% in cultural auxiliary production and intermediary services, and 2.5% in cultural consumption terminal production. By region, cultural industries in eastern and western regions achieved business revenue of 15,528.0 billion yuan and 2,029.8 billion yuan, up by 9.5% and 9.9% year on year, respectively, both higher than the national average growth rate. The central and northeastern regions achieved business revenue of 3,007.1 billion yuan and 260.5 billion yuan, up by 4.7% and 4.8% year on year. i. Total assets of cultural industries kept rising At the end of 2025, the total assets of Chinaâs cultural industries reached 37,447.4 billion yuan, an increase of 8.9% over the end of the previous year. By industry type, total assets of culture-related manufacturing, culture-related wholesale and retail, and culture-related services were 5,060.2 billion yuan, 2,744.3 billion yuan and 29,642.8 billion yuan, respectively, up by 5.7%, 7.2% and 9.6% over the end of the previous year. articles.title: 4.Profits of Industrial Enterprises above the Designated Size from January to May in 2026
articles.extracted_text: From January to May, the total profits of industrial enterprises above the designated size nationwide reached 3,143.96 billion yuan, a year-on-year increase of 18.8% (calculated on a comparable basis, see Annotation 2 for details). From January to May, among industrial enterprises above the designated size, state-holding enterprises realized a total profit of 1,048.66 billion yuan, a year-on-year increase of 19.6%; share-holding enterprises realized a total profit of 2,434.81 billion yuan, an increase of 24.1%; enterprises funded by foreign investors and investors from Hong Kong, Macao and Taiwan realized a total profit of 695.72 billion yuan, an increase of 4.2%; private enterprises realized a total profit of 772.65 billion yuan, an increase of 10.7%. From January to May, the mining industry realized a total profit of 479.52 billion yuan, a year-on-year increase of 33.5%; the manufacturing industry realized a total profit of 2,328.52 billion yuan, an increase of 20.0%; the production and supply of electricity, heat power, gas and water realized a total profit of 335.92 billion yuan, a decrease of 2.7%. From January to May, the profits of main industries were as follows: the profit of the smelting and pressing of non-ferrous metals increased by 117.1% year on year, that of the manufacture of computers, communication equipment and other electronic equipment increased by 103.9%, that of the manufacture of raw chemical materials and chemical products increased by 71.6%, that of the mining and washing of coal increased by 33.5%, that of the extraction of petroleum and natural gas increased by 17.2%, that of the textile industry increased by 11.7%, that of the manufacture of general-purpose machinery decreased by 0.2%, that of the production and supply of electricity and heat power decreased by 4.1%, that of the manufacture of special-purpose machinery decreased by 5.5%, that of the processing of food from agricultural and sideline products decreased by 13.3%, that of the manufacture of electrical machinery and apparatus decreased by 13.7%, that of the manufacture of automobiles decreased by 19.8%, that of the smelting and pressing of ferrous metals decreased by 37.4%, that of the manufacture of non-metallic mineral products decreased by 48.9%, and the processing of petroleum, coal and other fuels turned from loss to profit. From January to May, industrial enterprises above the designated size achieved a business revenue of 56.55 trillion yuan, a year-on-year increase of 5.5%; the operating costs incurred were 48.04 trillion yuan, an increase of 4.7%; the profit rate of business revenue was 5.56%, a year-on-year increase of 0.63 percentage points. At the end of May, the assets of industrial enterprises above the designated size totaled 193.55 trillion yuan, a year-on-year increase of 5.8%; the liabilities totaled 112.69 trillion yuan, an increase of 6.5%; the ownersâ equity totaled 80.87 trillion yuan, an increase of 5.0%; the asset-liability ratio was 58.2%, a year-on-year increase of 0.3 percentage points. At the end of May, the accounts receivable of industrial enterprises above the designated size totaled 28.17 trillion yuan, a year-on-year increase of 7.7%; the inventory of finished goods was 7.14 trillion yuan, an increase of 8.8%. From January to May, the costs for per-hundred-yuan business revenue of industrial enterprises above the designated size were 84.95 yuan, a year-on-year decrease of 0.59 yuan; the expenses for per-hundred-

articles.title: 5.Market Prices of Important Means of Production in Circulation, June 11-20, 2026
articles.extracted_text: China Economic Monitoring and Analysis Center In mid-June 2026, according to the monitoring of market prices of 50 kinds of important means of production under 9 categories in the national circulation field, the prices of 15 products increased, 32 kinds decreased, and 3 kinds remained flat compared with those in early June 2026. Market Prices of Important Means of Production in Circulation Ordinary Hot-Rolled Sheet (4.75-11.5mm, Q235) Pure Benzene (Petroleum Benzene, Industrial Grade) Polyethylene (LLDPE, Melt Index 2 Film Material) Lithium Iron Phosphate (Standard Power Grade) Gasoline (95#, National VI Emission Standard) Diesel Oil (0#, National VI Emission Standard) Shanxi Superior Mixed Coal (5500 kilocalories) Ordinary Portland Cement (P.O 42.5 in Bulk) VII. Agricultural Products (Mainly Used for Processing) Cotton (Ginned Cotton, White Cotton Grade III) Soybean Meal (Crude Protein Contentâ¥43%) Phosphate Fertilizer (55% Monoammonium Phosphate) Potassium Fertilizer (62% White Muriate of Potash, Port Price) Compound Fertilizer (Compound Fertilizer of Potassium Sulfate, 45% NPK) Pesticide (Glyphosate, 95% Technical Material) Notes: The previous period was June 1-10, 2026. Market prices of important means of production in circulation refer to the wholesale prices and selling prices of enterprises trading important means of production. Different from producer prices, market prices of means of production include producer prices, as well as circulation expenses, profits, and taxes of the enterprises. Producer prices and market prices influence each other. Due to a time lag, their movements may show divergence in a certain period of time. The monitoring of market prices of important means of production in circulation includes the prices of 50 kinds of products under 9 categories. For details of product categories and specifications, please see the attached table. The monitoring covers nearly 2,000 wholesalers, agents, and dealers of more than 300 trading markets in 31 provinces (autonomous regions and municipalities). Price monitoring methods include information officers gathering prices on-site or requesting prices by telephone, instant messaging tools, e-mail, and so on. The number of products with price increases, decreases, and no change is counted based on the percentage change (%). articles.title: 6.Industrial Production Operation in May 2026
articles.extracted_text: In May, the total value added of industrial enterprises above the designated size increased by 4.5% year on year in real terms (the growth rates of value added are the real growth rates after deducting price factors), 0.4 percentage points higher than the previous month. On a month-on-month basis, in May, the total value added of industrial enterprises above the designated size increased by 0.40% over the previous month. From January to May, the total value added of industrial enterprises above the designated size increased by 5.4% year on year. In terms of three sectors, in May, the value added of the mining industry increased by 2.3% year on year, that of the manufacturing industry increased by 4.4%, and that of the production and supply of electricity, heat power, gas and water increased by 7.6%. In terms of economic types, in May, the value added of state-holding enterprises increased by 3.7% year on year; that of share-holding enterprises increased by 5.2%; that of enterprises funded by foreign investors and investors from Hong Kong, Macao, and Taiwan increased by 1.9%; and that of private enterprises increased by 2.7%. In terms of industries, in May, the value added of 28 out of 41 major industries continued to show year-on-year growth. Among them, the value added of the mining and washing of coal went up by 3.5%, that of the extraction of petroleum and natural gas up by 1.5%, that of the processing of food from agricultural and sideline products up by 1.5%, that of the manufacture of liquor, beverages and refined tea down by 2.7%, that of the textile industry up by 2.6%, that of the manufacture of raw chemical materials and chemical products up by 0.3%, that of the manufacture of non-metallic mineral products down by 5.6%, that of the smelting and pressing of ferrous metals up by 1.6%, that of the smelting and pressing of non-ferrous metals down by 4.5%, that of the manufacture of general-purpose machinery up by 6.7%, that of the manufacture of special-purpose machinery up by 9.1%, that of the manufacture of automobiles up by 8.3%, that of the manufacture of railway, ship, aerospace and other transport equipment up by 7.4%, that of the manufacture of electrical machinery and apparatus up by 4.7%, that of the manufacture of computers, communication equipment and other electronic equipment up by 17.0%, and that of the production and supply of electricity and heat power up by 8.7%. In terms of products, in May, the output of 300 out of 626 products produced by industrial enterprises above the designated size increased year on year. Among them, the output of rolled steel was 123.03 million tons, down by 2.8% year on year; that of cement was 149.91 million tons, down by 8.1%; that of ten kinds of non-ferrous metals was 6.98 million tons, up by 2.2%; that of ethylene was 3.38 million tons, up by 2.1%; that of motor vehicles was 2.582 million sets, down by 3.2%, including 1.489 million new energy vehicles, up by 17.8%; the electricity generation was 784.3 billion kWh, up by 4.2%; and the crude oil processing volume was 53.72 million tons, down by 9.1%. In May, the product sales ratio of industrial enterprises above the designated size was 96.0%, a year-on-year decrease of 0.1 percentage points; and the export delivery value realized by industrial enterprises above the designated size was 1,388.4 billion yuan, a nominal increase of 10.1% year on year. articles.title: 7.Investment in Fixed Assets from January to May 2026
articles.extracted_text: From January to May 2026, the national investment in fixed assets (excluding rural households) was 17,851.2 billion yuan, a year-on-year decrease of 4.1% (calculated on a comparable basis, see Annotation 7 for details). Specifically, the investment in intellectual property products increased by 9.3% year on year. By industry, the investment in the primary industry was 388.7 billion yuan, a year-on-year increase of 5.9%; the investment in the secondary industry was 6,498.9 billion yuan, up 0.1%; and the investment in the tertiary industry was 10,963.6 billion yuan, down 6.8%. The industrial investment increased by 0.1% year on year. Specifically, the investment in mining increased by 6.2%, the investment in manufacturing decreased by 0.4%, and the investment in production and supply of electricity, heat power, gas and water decreased by 0.1%. The investment in infrastructure (see Annotation 1 for details of statistical scope) increased by 0.6% year on year. Specifically, the investment in waterway transportation increased by 23.3%, the investment in civil aviation transportation increased by 21.7%, the investment in ecological conservation and environmental governance increased by 3.5%, and the investment in the production and supply of gas increased by 2.8%. By region, the investment in the eastern region decreased by 6.4% year on year, the investment in the central region decreased by 2.9%, the investment in the western region decreased by 6.6%, and the investment in the northeastern region decreased by 17.5%. By statistical category of registration, the investment in fixed assets of domestic-invested enterprises decreased by 3.9% year on year, the investment in fixed assets of enterprises funded by investors from Hong Kong, Macao, and Taiwan decreased by 8.7%, and the investment in fixed assets of foreign-invested enterprises decreased by 4.3%. From January to May 2026, non-governmental investment in fixed assets decreased by 7.1% year on year. On a month-on-month basis, the investment in fixed assets (excluding rural households) decreased by 1.91% in May. Investment in Fixed Assets (Excluding Rural Households) from January to May 2026 Investment in Fixed Assets (Excluding Rural Households) Agriculture, forestry, animal husbandry and fishery Of which: Processing of food from agricultural and sideline products Manufacture of raw chemical materials and chemical products Smelting and pressing of non-ferrous metals Manufacture of railway, ship, aerospace and other transport equipment Manufacture of electrical machinery and apparatus Manufacture of computers, communication equipment and other electronic equipment Production and supply of electricity, heat power, gas and water Water conservancy, environment and public facilities management Of which: Management of water conservancy Enterprises funded by investors from Hong Kong, Macao, and Taiwan Note: The growth rates in this table are not adjusted for price factors. Investment in fixed assets (excluding rural households): It is a general term representing the monetary value of the workload completed in a certain period concerning the construction and acquisition of fixed assets, as well as the related expenses. articles.title: 8.Investment in Real Estate Development from January to May 2026
articles.extracted_text: I. The Completion of Investment in Real Estate Development From January to May, the investment in real estate development was 3,035.6 billion yuan, a year-on-year decrease of 16.2% (calculated on a comparable basis, see Annotation 5 for details), of which the investment in residential buildings was 2,342.6 billion yuan, down 15.6%. From January to May, the floor space of buildings under construction by real estate development enterprises was 5,487.75 million square meters, a year-on-year decrease of 12.3%. Specifically, the floor space of residential buildings under construction was 3,808.30 million square meters, down 12.6%. The floor space of buildings newly started was 179.29 million square meters, down 22.6%. Specifically, the floor space of residential buildings newly started was 130.84 million square meters, down 23.4%. The floor space of buildings completed was 140.87 million square meters, down 23.4%. Specifically, the floor space of residential buildings completed was 99.99 million square meters, down 25.0%. II. Sales of Newly Built Commercial Buildings From January to May, the floor space of newly built commercial buildings sold was 313.20 million square meters, a year-on-year decrease of 10.8%, of which the floor space of residential buildings sold decreased by 12.1%. The sales of newly built commercial buildings were 2,936.6 billion yuan, down 13.5%, of which the sales of residential buildings decreased by 14.1%. At the end of May, the floor space of commercial buildings for sale was 771.82 million square meters, down 0.4% year on year. Specifically, the floor space for sale held for less than three years was 571.52 million square meters, down 2.8%. III. Funds for Investment This Year for Real Estate Development Enterprises From January to May, the funds for investment this year for real estate development enterprises were 3,275.6 billion yuan, a year-on-year decrease of 19.0%. Among them, domestic loans were 487.5 billion yuan, down 28.7%; self-raised funds were 1,198.5 billion yuan, down 13.0%; deposits and advance receipts were 1,001.2 billion yuan, down 16.1%; individual mortgage loans were 406.6 billion yuan, down 28.0%. articles.title: 9.Total Retail Sales of Consumer Goods from January to May 2026
articles.extracted_text: From January to May, the total retail sales of consumer goods reached 20,603.1 billion yuan, up by 1.4% year on year. Specifically, the retail sales of consumer goods excluding automobiles reached 19,002.2 billion yuan, up by 2.7%. In May, the total retail sales of consumer goods reached 4,109.0 billion yuan, down by 0.6% year on year. Specifically, the retail sales of consumer goods excluding automobiles reached 3,778.1 billion yuan, up by 1.1%. By business area, from January to May, the retail sales of consumer goods in urban areas reached 17,866.2 billion yuan, up by 1.2% year on year; those in rural areas were 2,736.9 billion yuan, up by 2.6%. In May, the retail sales of consumer goods in urban areas reached 3,574.1 billion yuan, down by 0.9% year on year; those in rural areas were 534.9 billion yuan, up by 1.5%. By consumption pattern, from January to May, the retail sales of goods were 18,254.3 billion yuan, up by 1.2% year on year; the income of the catering industry was 2,348.8 billion yuan, up by 3.1%. In May, the retail sales of goods were 3,648.5 billion yuan, down by 0.7% year on year; the income of the catering industry was 460.5 billion yuan, up by 0.6%. By retail format, from January to May, the retail sales of convenience stores and supermarkets among retail enterprises (units) above the designated size increased by 6.8% and 3.6% year on year, respectively; those of specialty stores, department stores and brand-exclusive stores decreased by 1.2%, 1.8% and 7.6%, respectively. From January to May, Chinaâs online retail sales of goods and services reached 8,317.7 billion yuan, up by 5.9% year on year. Specifically, the online retail sales of goods were 5,271.8 billion yuan, up by 5.0%; among the online retail sales of goods, those of food, clothing, and daily necessities increased by 15.5%, 7.2%, and 1.6%, respectively. The online retail sales of services were 3,045.9 billion yuan, up by 7.6%. Total Retail Sales of Consumer Goods in May 2026 Of which: Retail sales of consumer goods excluding automobiles Of which: Retail sales of enterprises (units) above the designated size Of which: Income of the catering industry by enterprises (units) above the designated size Of which: Retail sales of goods by enterprises (units) above the designated size Household appliances and audio-video equipment Traditional Chinese and Western medicines Note: The growth rates in this table are not adjusted for price factors. Online retail sales of goods and services refer to the total retail sales of goods and services through public online trading platforms (including self-built websites and third-party platforms). articles.title: 10.Energy Production in May 2026
articles.extracted_text: In May 2026, the raw coal production by industrial enterprises above the designated size remained at a relatively high level, the growth of crude oil production was steady, the growth of natural gas production fell slightly, and the growth of electricity generation accelerated. I. Raw Coal, Crude Oil and Natural Gas Production and Related Information Raw coal production remained at a relatively high level. In May, the raw coal production by industrial enterprises above the designated size was 400 million tons, down by 1.7% year on year; the average daily output was 12.81 million tons. From January to May, the raw coal production by industrial enterprises above the designated size was 1.98 billion tons, a year-on-year decrease of 0.3%. The growth of crude oil production was steady. In May, the crude oil production by industrial enterprises above the designated size was 18.57 million tons, a year-on-year increase of 0.5%, 0.7 percentage points lower than that in April; the average daily output was 599 thousand tons. From January to May, the crude oil production by industrial enterprises above the designated size was 91.31 million tons, a year-on-year increase of 1.1%. The decline in the processing volume of crude oil expanded. In May, the processing volume of crude oil by industrial enterprises above the designated size was 53.72 million tons, a year-on-year decrease of 9.1%, with the decline expanded by 3.3 percentage points from April; the average daily processing volume was 1.733 million tons. From January to May, the processing volume of crude oil by industrial enterprises above the designated size was 292.80 million tons, a year-on-year decrease of 2.2%. Natural gas production fell slightly. In May, the production of natural gas by industrial enterprises above the designated size was 21.7 billion cubic meters, a year-on-year decrease of 2.2%, compared with a 1.9% increase in April; the average daily output was 700 million cubic meters. From January to May, the production of natural gas by industrial enterprises above the designated size was 111.7 billion cubic meters, a year-on-year increase of 1.7%. Electricity generation by industrial enterprises above the designated size grew faster. In May, electricity generation by industrial enterprises above the designated size was 784.3 billion kWh, a year-on-year increase of 4.2%, with the growth rate 1.6 percentage points higher than that in April; the average daily electricity generation was 25.30 billion kWh. From January to May, electricity generation by industrial enterprises above the designated size was 3,912.9 billion kWh, a year-on-year increase of 3.6%. By category, in May, the growth of thermal power generation by industrial enterprises above the designated size slowed down, while that of hydropower and solar power generation accelerated; the growth of nuclear power and wind power generation turned from decline to growth. Among them, thermal power generation by industrial enterprises above the designated size increased by 2.1% year on year, with the growth rate 1.0 percentage point lower than that in April; hydropower generation increased by 13.0%, with the growth rate 0.8 percentage points higher than that in April; nuclear power generation increased by 5.0%, compared with an 8.7% decline in April; wind power generation increased by 0.5%, compared with a 5.0% decline in April; solar power generation increased by 12.1%, with the growth rate 5.0 percentage points higher than that i

articles.title: 11.National Economy Showed Steady Momentum with Innovation-led and High-quality Developmen...
articles.extracted_text: In May, under the strong leadership of the Central Committee of the Communist Party of China (CPC) with Comrade Xi Jinping at its core, all regions and departments fully implemented the decisions and arrangements made by the CPC Central Committee and the State Council, adhered to the general principle of pursuing progress while ensuring stability, fully and faithfully applied the new development philosophy on all fronts, moved faster to foster a new pattern of development, strictly implemented more proactive and effective macro policies, and effectively addressed external shocks and challenges. As a result, production and supply registered steady progress, employment and prices were generally stable, foreign trade demonstrated strong resilience, and new growth drivers witnessed robust development. The national economy maintained steady momentum with innovation-led and high-quality development. 1. Industrial Production Witnessed Accelerated Growth and Equipment Manufacturing and High-Tech Manufacturing Grew Fast. In May, the total value added of industrial enterprises above the designated size grew by 4.5 percent year on year, 0.4 percentage points faster than that of the previous month; the month-on-month growth was 0.40 percent. In terms of sectors, the value added of mining increased by 2.3 percent year on year, that of manufacturing grew by 4.4 percent, and that of production and supply of electricity, heat power, gas and water went up by 7.6 percent. The value added of equipment manufacturing increased by 9.5 percent year on year and that of high-tech manufacturing increased by 15.1 percent, 1.2 percentage points and 2.3 percentage points faster than that of the previous month respectively. An analysis by types of ownership showed that the value added of state holding enterprises was up by 3.7 percent year on year; that of share-holding enterprises was up by 5.2 percent; that of enterprises funded by foreign investors or investors from Hong Kong, Macao and Taiwan was up by 1.9 percent; and that of private enterprises was up by 2.7 percent. In terms of products, the production of 3D printing devices, lithium-ion batteries and industrial robots grew by 54.4 percent, 40.0 percent and 27.9 percent year on year respectively. In the first five months, the value added of industrial enterprises above the designated size went up by 5.4 percent year on year. In May, the Manufacturing Purchasing Managersâ Index stood at 50.0 percent. The Production and Operation Expectation Index was 53.9 percent. In the first four months, the total profits made by industrial enterprises above the designated size were 2,435.8 billion yuan, up by 18.2 percent year on year. 2. Service Sector Grew Steadily and Modern Services Showed Good Momentum of Development. In May, the Index of Services Production grew by 4.4 percent year on year, 0.1 percentage points faster than that of the previous month. Specifically, that of information transmission, software and information technology services, leasing and business services, finance, and transport, storage and postal services grew by 11.3 percent, 10.9 percent, 7.0 percent and 4.8 percent year on year respectively. In the first five months, the Index of Services Production increased by 4.8 percent year on year. In the first four months, the business revenue of service enterprises above the designated size grew by 6.4 percent year on year.
